error.signature: WRONGTYPE Operation against a key holding the wrong kind of value

verdict.summary: Command used on a key that holds a different data type than expected. E.g., GET on a hash key, or LPUSH on a string key.

dead_ends.action: Delete and recreate the key with the correct type
dead_ends.why_fails: Destroys existing data. Other parts of the application may depend on the current type.

dead_ends.action: Use RENAME to work around the type check
dead_ends.why_fails: RENAME does not change the type; the renamed key still has the wrong type for the operation

workarounds.action: Check key type before operating on it
workarounds.how: redis-cli TYPE mykey  # returns string, list, set, zset, hash, stream

workarounds.action: Fix the application code to use correct commands for the data type
workarounds.how: String: GET/SET; Hash: HGET/HSET; List: LPUSH/LRANGE; Set: SADD/SMEMBERS; Sorted Set: ZADD/ZRANGE

workarounds.action: Use separate key names or namespacing for different data types
